channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The best free cloud computing courses in 2026
item.description: The cloud isn’t new, but the way enterprises use it keeps evolving. AI workloads, new platform services, and tighter governance mean teams can’t rely on outdated knowledge. The real issue isn’t ignorance but drift.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The Future of Critical Infrastructure: Strategies for IT/OT Security, Workforce Readiness, and Trust
item.description: During this webinar, government and industry experts will discuss how agencies are addressing IT/OT security and resilience through workforce development, pilot programs and emerging technologies.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: When government stops looking ahead, risks multiply: New survey shows foresight fading fast
item.description: "I think this is really an essential next step as we look to continuing the transformation that's going on in the government right now," Cunzeman said.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gerton, Federal News Network

item.title: New PDF compression filter will save space, need software updates
item.description: Brotli is one of the most widely used but least-known compression formats ever devised, long incorporated into all major browsers and web content delivery networks (CDNs).
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: From Meta to Samsung, global tech unleashes spending to chase AI
item.description: The world's largest tech firms show no signs of easing up on AI spending, a record wave that's propelling hardware providers like Samsung Electronics Co. and SK Hynix Inc.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: How to reduce Windows driver bloat
item.description: Over time on Windows systems, it’s inevitable that old device drivers get supplanted by new ones. Even on systems where drivers aren’t rigorously maintained, Windows Update will typically update at least a dozen drivers annually.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: What is prompt engineering? The art of AI orchestration
item.description: Prompt engineering is the process of crafting inputs, or prompts, to a generative AI system that lead to the system producing better outputs.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Big Tech CEOs’ tortured statements on ICE in Minnesota show an industry captured by Trump
item.description: On the morning of January 24 in downtown Minneapolis, an ICE agent shot and killed protester Alex Pretti, an ICU nurse at a local Veterans Affairs hospital. Just 2 miles away, on January 7, another ICE agent had shot and killed Nicole Renee Good, a mother.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft 365: A guide to the updates
item.description: Microsoft 365 (and Office 365) subscribers get more frequent software updates than those who have purchased Office without a subscription, which means subscribers have access to the latest features, security patches, and bug fixes.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Nuclear safety rules quietly rewritten to favor AI
item.description: The US Department of Energy (DoE) program that would see three new experimental commercial nuclear reactors launched by July 4 is the driver behind the secret rewriting of nuclear safety and security standards, says a report released Wednesday by NPR.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Nuclear safety rules quietly rewritten to favor AI
item.description: The US Department of Energy (DoE) program that would see three new experimental commercial nuclear reactors launched by July 4 is the driver behind the secret rewriting of nuclear safety and security standards, says a report released Wednesday by NPR.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Crooks are hijacking and reselling AI infrastructure: Report
item.description: For years, CSOs have worried about their IT infrastructure being used for unauthorized cryptomining. Now, say researchers, they’d better start worrying about crooks hijacking and reselling access to exposed corporate AI infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft beats Wall Street expectations with $81.3B revenue
item.description: Microsoft reports revenue of $81.3 billion for the October-December quarter, marking a 17% increase from the previous year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Spanish soccer league offers reward of 50 euros for tips to ID bars illegally broadcasting games
item.description: The Spanish league says it is stepping up its fight against audio-visual fraud by offering 50 euros for each verified tip on the establishments broadcasting games without proper permission.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 11 cool things Copilot can do in Excel
item.description: Microsoft has woven its generative AI technology throughout Microsoft 365, the company’s productivity suite. Its Copilot AI assistant is most often used in M365 apps for text-oriented actions, such as generating email or document drafts in Outlook or Word, or writing summaries of Teams meetings.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Uber unveils a new robotaxi with no driver behind the wheel
item.description: Uber robotaxi testing begins on public roads in San Francisco Bay Area with plans to launch driverless rides in a major U.S. city later in 2026.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Microsoft launches its second generation AI inference chip, Maia 200
item.description: Signaling that the future of AI may not just be how many tokens an AI model generates, but how optimally it does so, Microsoft has announced Maia 200, which it described as a breakthrough inference accelerator and inference powerhouse.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Why clicking the wrong Copilot link could put your data at risk
item.description: Microsoft Copilot security vulnerability allows attackers to steal data through malicious links. Learn how the attack works, and protect yourself.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
